View this page

layers¶

layers.classification_heads¶

Classification Heads Module.

A collection of classification head architectures designed to map LLM or encoder output representations to class logits for time-series classification tasks. Each head accepts patch embeddings of shape (B, N, H) and returns logits of shape (B, K), where:

  • B – batch size

  • N – number of patches

  • H – LLM hidden dimensionality (e.g. 768 for GPT-2)

  • K – number of target classes

Example

>>> x = torch.randn(8, 32, 768) # Patch embedding
>>> head = ClassificationHeadDeepRange(
...     llm_hidden_size=768,
...     num_patches=32,
...     num_classes=6,
...     dropout=0.1,
...     activation="gelu"
... )
>>> logits = head(x)
>>> print(logits.shape)
torch.Size([8, 6])
class layers.classification_heads.ClassificationHeadDeepRange(llm_hidden_size: int, num_patches: int, num_classes: int, dropout: float, activation: str)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Classification head for the DeepRange model.

Flattens patch embeddings, applies layer normalization, a configurable activation function, dropout, and a linear projection to class logits.

References

Paper

Chen, Y. et al.: LLMs are few-shot multivariate time series classifiers (2025) Chen, Yakun; Li, Zihao; Yang, Chao; Wang, Xianzhi; Xu, Guandong: LLMs are few-shot multivariate time series classifiers, in: Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery, Vol. 39, pp. 66, 2025

Source Code

https://github.com/junekchen/llm-fewshot-mtsc/blob/main/LLMFew.py

llm_hidden_size¶

Hidden dimensionality of the LLM output.

Type:

int

num_patches¶

Number of patch embeddings per sample.

Type:

int

num_classes¶

Number of target classes.

Type:

int

dropout¶

Dropout layer.

Type:

nn.Dropout

activation¶

Instantiated activation function module. Supported values: 'gelu', 'relu', 'leaky_relu'.

Type:

nn.Module

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Computes class logits from patch embeddings.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Patch embeddings of shape (B, N, H) where B is the batch size, N is the number of patches, and H is llm_hidden_size.

Returns:

Class logits of shape (B, K).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.classification_heads.ClassificationHeadLetsC(input_dim: int, num_classes: int, num_cnn_blocks: int, cnn_channels: int, kernel_size: int, mlp_hidden: int, dropout: float, pooling_type: str, use_batch_norm: bool)[source]¶

Bases: Module

CNN-based classification head for the LetsC model.

Applies a stack of 1D convolutional blocks to patch embeddings for local feature extraction, followed by global pooling and an MLP classifier. This avoids flattening the sequence dimension, making it robust to varying sequence lengths.

References

Paper

Kaur, Rachneet, et al. “LETS-C: Leveraging Text Embedding for Time Series Classification.” Proceedings of the 63rd Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (Volume 1: Long Papers). 2025.

input_dim¶

Input feature dimensionality (H from patch embeddings).

Type:

int

num_classes¶

Number of target classes.

Type:

int

num_cnn_blocks¶

Number of Conv1d blocks to stack.

Type:

int

cnn_channels¶

Number of output channels for each Conv1d block.

Type:

int

kernel_size¶

Kernel size for Conv1d layers.

Type:

int

mlp_hidden¶

Hidden dimensionality of the MLP classifier.

Type:

int

dropout¶

Dropout probability applied in CNN blocks and MLP.

Type:

float

pooling_type¶

Global pooling strategy. Supported values: 'avg' (adaptive average), 'max' (adaptive max).

Type:

str

use_batch_norm¶

If True, inserts BatchNorm1d after each Conv1d layer and disables conv bias.

Type:

bool

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Computes class logits via CNN feature extraction and global pooling.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Patch embeddings of shape (B, N, H) where B is the batch size, N is the number of patches, and H is the input feature dimensionality.

Returns:

Class logits of shape (B, K).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.classification_heads.ClassificationHeadOFA(llm_hidden_size: int, num_patches: int, num_classes: int, activation: str)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Classification head for the One-Fits-All (OFA) model.

Flattens patch embeddings, applies layer normalization, and projects to class logits via a single linear layer. An activation function is applied to the raw patch embeddings before flattening.

References

Paper

Zhou, Tian; Niu, Peisong; Wang, Xue; Sun, Liang; Jin, Rong: One fits all: power general time series analysis by pretrained LM, in: Proceedings of the 37th International Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems, 2023

Source Code

https://github.com/DAMO-DI-ML/NeurIPS2023-One-Fits-All/blob/main/Classification/src/models/gpt4ts.py

llm_hidden_size¶

Hidden dimensionality of the LLM output.

Type:

int

num_patches¶

Number of patch embeddings per sample.

Type:

int

num_classes¶

Number of target classes.

Type:

int

activation¶

Name of the activation function. Supported values: 'gelu', 'relu', 'leaky_relu'.

Type:

str

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Computes class logits from patch embeddings.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Patch embeddings of shape (B, N, H).

Returns:

Class logits of shape (B, K).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

Raises:

ValueError – If activation is not one of 'gelu', 'relu', or 'leaky_relu'.

class layers.classification_heads.ClassificationHeadTimeLLM(llm_hidden_size: int, num_patches: int, num_classes: int, dropout: float, activation: str)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Classification head for the Time-LLM model.

Flattens patch embeddings, applies layer normalization, dropout, and an activation function, then projects to class logits via a linear layer.

References

Paper

Jin, M. et al.: Reprogramming Large Language Models (2024) Jin, Ming; Wang, Shiyu; Ma, Lintao; Chu, Zhixuan; Zhang, James Y.; Shi, Xiaoming; Chen, Pin-Yu; Liang, Yuxuan; Li, Yuan-Fang; Pan, Shirui; Wen, Qingsong: Time-LLM: Time Series Forecasting by Reprogramming Large Language Models, in: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations, 2024

Source Code

https://github.com/KimMeen/Time-LLM/blob/main/models/TimeLLM.py

llm_hidden_size¶

Hidden dimensionality of the LLM output.

Type:

int

num_patches¶

Number of patch embeddings per sample.

Type:

int

num_classes¶

Number of target classes.

Type:

int

dropout¶

Dropout layer.

Type:

nn.Dropout

activation¶

Name of the activation function. Supported values: 'gelu', 'relu', 'leaky_relu'.

Type:

str

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Computes class logits from patch embeddings.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Patch embeddings of shape (B, N, H).

Returns:

Class logits of shape (B, K).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

Raises:

ValueError – If activation is not one of 'gelu', 'relu', or 'leaky_relu'.

layers.patching¶

Patching Module.

Provides a patching layer that segments multivariate time series into overlapping or non-overlapping patches for patch-based transformer models.

Example

>>> patching = Patching(patch_length=16, patch_stride=8)
>>> x = torch.randn(8, 512, 7)   # [B, T, C]
>>> out = patching(x)            # [B, C, num_patches, patch_length]
class layers.patching.Patching(patch_lenght: int, patch_stride: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Segments a multivariate time series into fixed-length patches.

Applies replication padding to the time axis before unfolding the sequence into patches of length patch_length with stride patch_stride. Operates channel-independently.

patch_length¶

Length of each patch.

Type:

int

patch_stride¶

Stride between consecutive patches.

Type:

int

padding_patch_layer¶

Replication padding applied along the time axis before unfolding.

Type:

nn.ReplicationPad1d

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Segments the input time series into patches.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Multivariate time series of shape (B, T, C) where B is the batch size, T is the sequence length, and C is the number of channels.

Returns:

Patch tensor of shape

(B, C, num_patches, patch_length).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

layers.patchwise_temporal_convolution_encoder¶

Patchwise Temporal Convolution Encoder Module.

Provides a causal CNN encoder that processes time-series patches channel-independently using dilated causal convolutions with residual connections, followed by adaptive pooling and a linear projection.

  • B – batch size

  • T – sequence lenght

  • C – number of channels (features)

References

Paper

Chen, Y. et al.: LLMs are few-shot multivariate time series classifiers (2025) Chen, Yakun; Li, Zihao; Yang, Chao; Wang, Xianzhi; Xu, Guandong: LLMs are few-shot multivariate time series classifiers, in: Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery, Vol. 39, pp. 66, 2025

Source Code

https://github.com/junekchen/llm-fewshot-mtsc/blob/main/CasualCNN.py

Example

>>> x = torch.randn(8, 3, 16, 64) # (B, C, num_patches, patch_length)
>>> encoder = CausalCNNEncoder(
...     in_channels=3,
...     channels=32,
...     depth=4,
...     reduced_size=128,
...     out_channels=256,
...     kernel_size=3,
... )
>>> embeddings = encoder(x)
>>> print(embeddings.shape)
torch.Size([128, 256])
class layers.patchwise_temporal_convolution_encoder.CausalCNN(in_channels: int, channels: int, depth: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

A stack of dilated causal convolution blocks with exponentially growing dilation.

Builds a causal CNN by stacking CausalConvolutionBlock layers where the dilation doubles at each depth level, allowing the network to capture long-range temporal dependencies efficiently.

in_channels¶

Number of input channels.

Type:

int

channels¶

Number of channels in intermediate blocks.

Type:

int

depth¶

Number of intermediate causal convolution blocks. The total number of blocks is depth + 1.

Type:

int

out_channels¶

Number of output channels of the final block.

Type:

int

kernel_size¶

Kernel size for all non-residual convolutions.

Type:

int

network¶

Sequential stack of CausalConvolutionBlock layers.

Type:

nn.Sequential

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Passes the input through the causal CNN stack.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, in_channels, T).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, out_channels, T).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.patchwise_temporal_convolution_encoder.CausalCNNEncoder(in_channels: int, channels: int, depth: int, reduced_size: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Encodes time-series patches into fixed-size representations.

Applies a CausalCNN to each patch independently, reduces the time axis to a single value via adaptive max pooling, squeezes the singleton dimension, and projects to the output embedding size via a linear layer.

in_channels¶

Number of input channels.

Type:

int

channels¶

Number of channels in intermediate causal CNN blocks.

Type:

int

depth¶

Depth of the causal CNN.

Type:

int

reduced_size¶

Fixed length to which the causal CNN output is reduced before the linear projection.

Type:

int

out_channels¶

Number of output embedding dimensions.

Type:

int

kernel_size¶

Kernel size of the non-residual convolutions.

Type:

int

network¶

Sequential pipeline of causal CNN, adaptive max pooling, channel squeezing, and linear projection.

Type:

nn.Sequential

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Encodes a batch of time-series patches into fixed-size embeddings.

Reshapes the input to process each patch independently across the batch and channel dimensions, then applies the encoder network.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Patch tensor of shape (B, C, num_patches, patch_length) where B is the batch size, C is the number of channels, and num_patches is the number of patches.

Returns:

Encoded patch embeddings of shape

(B * num_patches, out_channels).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.patchwise_temporal_convolution_encoder.CausalConvolutionBlock(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: int, dilation: int, final: bool = False)[source]¶

Bases: Module

A residual block of two dilated causal convolutions.

Each block consists of two weight-normalised causal Conv1d layers with LeakyReLU activations, followed by a residual connection. If in_channels != out_channels, a 1×1 convolution is used to match dimensions. An optional final activation can be disabled for the last block in a network.

in_channels¶

Number of input channels.

Type:

int

out_channels¶

Number of output channels.

Type:

int

kernel_size¶

Kernel size of the non-residual convolutions.

Type:

int

dilation¶

Dilation factor of the non-residual convolutions.

Type:

int

final¶

If True, disables the final activation function after the residual addition. Defaults to False.

Type:

bool, optional

causal¶

Sequential block of two causal convolutions, chomping layers, and activations.

Type:

nn.Sequential

upordownsample¶

1×1 convolution for residual channel matching, or None if channels already match.

Type:

nn.Conv1d or None

relu¶

Final activation applied after the residual addition, or None if final=True.

Type:

nn.LeakyReLU or None

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Applies causal convolutions and residual connection.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, in_channels, T).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, out_channels, T).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.patchwise_temporal_convolution_encoder.Chomp1d(chomp_size: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Removes trailing elements from a 1D convolution output.

Used after padded causal convolutions to restore the original sequence length and ensure causality — no future information leaks into the current timestep.

chomp_size¶

Number of trailing elements to remove from the time axis.

Type:

int

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Removes trailing elements from the time axis.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, C, T).

Returns:

Tensor of shape (B, C, T - chomp_size).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.patchwise_temporal_convolution_encoder.SqueezeChannels[source]¶

Bases: Module

Squeezes the third dimension of a three-dimensional tensor.

Used after adaptive pooling to remove the singleton time dimension, converting (B, C, 1) to (B, C).

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Squeezes the third dimension of the input tensor.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, C, 1).

Returns:

Squeezed tensor of shape (B, C).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

layers.prompt_embedding¶

PromptEmbedding Module.

This module provides a class to generate prompt embeddings for time-series classification tasks using a pretrained LLM.

  • B – batch size

  • T – sequence lenght

  • C – number of channels (features)

Example

>>> x = torch.randn(8, 64, 3) # (B, T, C)
>>> prompt_module = PromptEmbedding(
...     llm=llm,
...     tokenizer=tokenizer,
...     task_description="Classify sensor signals",
...     prompt_max_lenght=128,
...     label=None,
...     device="cpu",
...     num_classes=5,
... )
>>>
>>> embeddings = prompt_module(x)
>>> print(embeddings.shape)
torch.Size([8, 128, 768])  # (batch, prompt_length, embedding_dim)

References

Paper

Jin, Ming; Wang, Shiyu; Ma, Lintao; Chu, Zhixuan; Zhang, James Y.; Shi, Xiaoming; Chen, Pin-Yu; Liang, Yuxuan; Li, Yuan-Fang; Pan, Shirui; Wen, Qingsong: Time-LLM: Time Series Forecasting by Reprogramming Large Language Models, in: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations, 2024

Source Code

https://github.com/KimMeen/Time-LLM/blob/main/models/TimeLLM.py

class layers.prompt_embedding.PromptEmbedding(llm: Module, tokenizer, task_description: str, prompt_max_lenght: int, label: list[str], device: str | device, num_classes: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Generates prompt embeddings.

This module creates textual prompts describing the classification task and converts them into embeddings using a pretrained LLM’s input embeddings.

llm¶

Pretrained language model providing embeddings.

Type:

nn.Module

tokenizer¶

Tokenizer compatible with the LLM.

task_description¶

Text description of the classification task.

Type:

str

prompt_max_lenght¶

Maximum token length for prompts.

Type:

int

label¶

Labels for the classification task (optional).

Type:

list[str]

device¶

Device to place tensors on (e.g., “cpu” or “cuda”).

Type:

str | torch.device

num_classes¶

Number of target classes.

Type:

int

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Computes the prompt embeddings for a batch of time-series inputs.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, T, C), where B is the batch size, T is the number of timesteps, C is the number of channels.

Returns:

Embedded prompts with shape corresponding to the

LLM input embeddings, typically (B, prompt_length, embedding_dim).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

scenario_classification_prompt(x: Tensor) → list[str][source]¶

Generates textual classification prompts for a batch of time-series data.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, T, C), where B is the batch size, T is the number of timesteps, C is the number of channels.

Returns:

List of prompt strings for each sample in the batch.

Return type:

list[str]

layers.reprogramming_layer¶

Reprogramming Layer Module.

Aligns time-series patch embeddings with natural-language representations to enable time-series reasoning in the LLM backbone.

Tensor dimension conventions used throughout this module:

  • B – batch size

  • N – number of patches

  • H – LLM hidden dimensionality

  • S – number of source token embeddings

  • h – number of attention heads

  • d – per-head feature dimension (H // h)

Example:

>>> layer = ReprogrammingLayer(llm_hidden_size=768, num_attention_heads=12)
>>> patches = torch.randn(8, 16, 768)     # [B, N, H]
>>> tok_emb = torch.randn(1000, 768)      # [S, H]
>>> out = layer(patches, tok_emb, tok_emb)
>>> out.shape
torch.Size([8, 16, 768])

References

Paper

Jin, Ming; Wang, Shiyu; Ma, Lintao; Chu, Zhixuan; Zhang, James Y.; Shi, Xiaoming; Chen, Pin-Yu; Liang, Yuxuan; Li, Yuan-Fang; Pan, Shirui; Wen, Qingsong: Time-LLM: Time Series Forecasting by Reprogramming Large Language Models, in: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations, 2024

Source Code

https://github.com/KimMeen/Time-LLM/blob/main/models/TimeLLM.py

class layers.reprogramming_layer.ReprogrammingLayer(llm_hidden_size: int, num_attention_heads: int, attention_dropout: float = 0.1)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Reprogramming layer using cross-attention to align patch embeddings with an LLM’s token space.

Projects time-series patch embeddings (queries) into the vocabulary space of a pre-trained LLM by attending over a set of source token embeddings (keys/values). This allows the LLM to process time-series patches as if they were natural-language tokens, without fine-tuning the LLM itself.

Parameters:

  • llm_hidden_size (int) – Hidden dimensionality of the target LLM (e.g. 768 for GPT-2, 4096 for LLaMA-7B). Must be divisible by num_attention_heads.

  • num_attention_heads (int) – Number of parallel attention heads. Each head operates on a subspace of size llm_hidden_size // num_attention_heads.

  • attention_dropout (float) – Dropout probability applied to the attention weight matrix before the weighted sum over values. Defaults to 0.1.

forward(target_embedding: Tensor, source_embedding: Tensor, value_embedding: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Reprojects time-series patch embeddings into the LLM token space via cross-attention.

Each patch embedding attends over all source token embeddings, producing a convex combination of their value projections. The result lies in the same space as the LLM’s input embeddings and can be forwarded directly through the frozen LLM.

Parameters:

  • target_embedding (torch.Tensor) – Patch embeddings acting as queries, of shape [B, N, H].

  • source_embedding (torch.Tensor) – Mapped LLM token embeddings acting as keys, of shape [S, H]. Shared across the batch (no leading batch dimension).

  • value_embedding (torch.Tensor) – Embeddings used to form the attended values, of shape [S, H]. In the original Time-LLM formulation this is identical to source_embedding, but kept separate to allow independent key/value sources.

Returns:

Reprogrammed patch representations of shape [B, N, H], matching target_embedding.

Return type:

torch.Tensor

layers.time_series_embeddings¶

Time Series Embeddings.

Maps the temporal or feature dimensions of a time series sequence into a higher-dimensional representation space (e.g., the hidden size of an LLM).

Tensor dimension conventions used throughout this module:

  • B: batch size

  • T: sequence length (time dimension)

  • C: number of channels / variables

  • N: number of extracted patches

  • P: patch length

  • H: embedding dimension (LLM hidden size)

References

Papers

Jin, Ming; Wang, Shiyu; Ma, Lintao; Chu, Zhixuan; Zhang, James Y.; Shi, Xiaoming; Chen, Pin-Yu; Liang, Yuxuan; Li, Yuan-Fang; Pan, Shirui; Wen, Qingsong: Time-LLM: Time Series Forecasting by Reprogramming Large Language Models, in: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations, 2024

Nie, Yuqi; Nguyen, Nam H.; Sinthong, Phanwadee; Kalagnanam, Jayant: A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers, in: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations, 2023

Source Codes

https://github.com/KimMeen/Time-LLM/blob/main/layers/Embed.py

https://github.com/yuqinie98/PatchTST/blob/main/PatchTST_supervised/layers/Embed.py

class layers.time_series_embeddings.FixedEmbedding(c_in: int, llm_hidden_size: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Non-trainable sinusoidal nn.Embedding initialised like positional encodings.

Builds an embedding table with sine/cosine values (identical formula to PositionalEmbedding) and freezes the weights so they are never updated during training. Intended as a drop-in replacement for nn.Embedding inside TemporalEmbedding.

Parameters:

  • c_in (int) – Vocabulary size, i.e., the number of discrete time categories (e.g., 24 for hours, 32 for days).

  • llm_hidden_size (int) – Embedding dimensionality H.

Example:

>>> fe = FixedEmbedding(c_in=24, llm_hidden_size=128)
>>> idx = torch.arange(24)
>>> fe(idx).shape
torch.Size([24, 128])
forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Looks up fixed sinusoidal embeddings for the given indices.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Integer index tensor of arbitrary shape.

Returns:

Detached embedding tensor with an extra trailing dimension of size H.

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.time_series_embeddings.PatchEmbedding(use_linear: bool, embed_type: str | None, freq: str | None, use_token: bool, use_positional: bool, use_temporal: bool, llm_hidden_size: int, patch_lenght: int, patch_stride: int, dropout: float, num_channels: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Embeds a multivariate time series into patch-level LLM-compatible representations.

Splits each channel’s time series into overlapping patches, optionally pads the sequence, and projects each patch into the LLM hidden space using either a linear layer or a token (convolutional) embedding. Sinusoidal positional encodings and temporal (calendar) embeddings can be added on top.

Parameters:

  • use_linear (bool) – If True, uses a single nn.Linear layer to project flattened patches of shape [patch_length * num_channels] into llm_hidden_size. Mutually exclusive with use_token.

  • embed_type (str | None) – Embedding variant for the optional temporal embedding. Use 'fixed' for sinusoidal-initialised nn.Embedding weights, 'learned' for standard nn.Embedding, or 'timeF' for a linear projection of continuous time features. Ignored when use_temporal is False or use_token is False.

  • freq (str | None) – Sampling frequency token that controls which calendar features are built. Supported values for TemporalEmbedding: 't' (minute), 'h' (hour, default). For TimeFeatureEmbedding the mapping is {'h': 4, 't': 5, 's': 6, 'm': 1, 'a': 1, 'w': 2, 'd': 3, 'b': 3}. Ignored when use_temporal is False or use_token is False.

  • use_token (bool) – If True, uses a causal TokenEmbedding (1-D circular convolution) instead of a linear projection. Mutually exclusive with use_linear.

  • use_positional (bool) – If True and use_token is True, adds sinusoidal positional encodings to the patch embeddings.

  • use_temporal (bool) – If True and use_token is True, adds calendar-aware temporal embeddings to the patch embeddings.

  • llm_hidden_size (int) – Target embedding dimensionality H; should match the hidden size of the downstream LLM.

  • patch_lenght (int) – Number of time steps P in each patch.

  • patch_stride (int) – Step size between consecutive patch start positions. Also used as the right-side replication padding length so that the last patch is always fully populated.

  • dropout (float) – Dropout probability applied to the final patch embeddings.

  • num_channels (int) – Number of input channels / variables C. Used to compute the flattened patch dimension P * C.

Raises:

ValueError – If both use_linear and use_token are True.

Example

>>> embedder = PatchEmbedding(
...     use_linear=True,
...     embed_type=None,
...     freq=None,
...     use_token=False,
...     use_positional=False,
...     use_temporal=False,
...     llm_hidden_size=768,
...     patch_lenght=16,
...     patch_stride=8,
...     dropout=0.1,
...     num_channels=4,
... )
>>> x = torch.randn(2, 128, 4)  # [B, T, C]
>>> embedder(x).shape
torch.Size([2, 16, 768])
forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Converts a raw multivariate time series into patch embeddings.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Raw time series of shape [B, T, C].

Returns:

Patch embeddings of shape [B, N, H], where N = floor((T + patch_stride - patch_length) / patch_stride) + 1 after padding.

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.time_series_embeddings.PositionalEmbedding(llm_hidden_size: int, max_lenght: int = 5000)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Fixed sinusoidal positional encoding.

Pre-computes a table of sine/cosine positional encodings up to max_length positions and reads off the first T rows at forward time. The encoding is stored as a non-trainable buffer.

Parameters:

  • llm_hidden_size (int) – Embedding dimensionality H. Must be even so that sine and cosine values can be interleaved across all dimensions.

  • max_lenght (int) – Maximum sequence length supported. Defaults to 5000.

Example:

>>> pe = PositionalEmbedding(llm_hidden_size=64)
>>> x = torch.zeros(2, 10, 64)  # [B, T, H]
>>> pe(x).shape
torch.Size([1, 10, 64])
forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Returns positional encodings for the first T positions.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape [B, T, H]. Only x.size(1) (the sequence length) is used; values are ignored.

Returns:

Positional encodings of shape [1, T, H], broadcastable over the batch dimension.

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.time_series_embeddings.TemporalEmbedding(llm_hidden_size: int, embed_type: str = 'fixed', freq: str = 'h')[source]¶

Bases: Module

Calendar-aware embedding built from discrete time-of-day / date indices.

Encodes up to five calendar fields — month, day-of-month, weekday, hour, and (optionally) minute — each via its own Embedding table, and sums them into a single dense vector. Supports either fixed sinusoidal weights (FixedEmbedding) or standard trainable weights (nn.Embedding).

The expected input layout along the last axis is:

x[..., 0] = month   (1–12, table size 13)
x[..., 1] = day     (1–31, table size 32)
x[..., 2] = weekday (0–6,  table size  7)
x[..., 3] = hour    (0–23, table size 24)
x[..., 4] = minute  (0–3,  table size  4)  # only when freq == 't'
Parameters:

  • llm_hidden_size (int) – Output embedding dimensionality H.

  • embed_type (str) – Selects the embedding variant. Use 'fixed' for frozen sinusoidal weights via FixedEmbedding, or any other string (e.g. 'learned') for standard nn.Embedding. Defaults to 'fixed'.

  • freq (str) – Sampling frequency. When set to 't', a minute-level embedding table is created in addition to the hourly and coarser ones. Defaults to 'h'.

Example:

>>> te = TemporalEmbedding(llm_hidden_size=64, embed_type='fixed', freq='h')
>>> # x contains [month, day, weekday, hour] indices, shape [B, T, 4]
>>> x = torch.randint(0, 12, (2, 10, 4))
>>> te(x).shape
torch.Size([2, 10, 64])
forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Computes the sum of all active calendar embeddings.

Casts input to long, looks up each calendar field in its own embedding table, and returns the element-wise sum.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Integer calendar-feature tensor of shape [B, T, F], where F >= 4 (F = 5 when freq == 't'). The column order must be [month, day, weekday, hour, (minute)].

Returns:

Summed temporal embedding of shape [B, T, H].

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.time_series_embeddings.TimeFeatureEmbedding(d_model: int, embed_type: str = 'timeF', freq: str = 'h')[source]¶

Bases: Module

Projects continuous time features into the embedding space with a linear layer.

Unlike TemporalEmbedding, which looks up discrete calendar indices, this module accepts the real-valued time features produced by tsai / GluonTS time_features_from_frequency_str helpers and projects them linearly into llm_hidden_size.

The number of input features d_inp is determined by the freq argument via the lookup table:

{'h': 4, 't': 5, 's': 6, 'm': 1, 'a': 1, 'w': 2, 'd': 3, 'b': 3}
Parameters:

  • d_model (int) – Output embedding dimensionality H.

  • embed_type (str) – Included for API consistency with TemporalEmbedding; the value is not used internally. Defaults to 'timeF'.

  • freq (str) – Sampling frequency string that controls the number of continuous time features. Defaults to 'h' (4 features).

Example:

>>> tfe = TimeFeatureEmbedding(d_model=128, freq='h')
>>> x = torch.randn(2, 10, 4)  # [B, T, d_inp=4 for freq='h']
>>> tfe(x).shape
torch.Size([2, 10, 128])
forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Linearly projects continuous time features to the embedding space.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Continuous time-feature tensor of shape [B, T, d_inp], where d_inp matches the value associated with freq in the frequency map.

Returns:

Projected embeddings of shape [B, T, H].

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class layers.time_series_embeddings.TokenEmbedding(c_in: int, llm_hidden_size: int)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Projects patch tokens into the embedding space with a 1-D convolution.

Uses a circular-padded Conv1d with kernel size 3 to mix neighbouring time steps within each token, providing a richer inductive bias than a plain linear layer. Weights are initialised with Kaiming normal (fan-in, leaky ReLU).

Parameters:

  • c_in (int) – Number of input features per time step (i.e., the flattened patch dimension P * C).

  • llm_hidden_size (int) – Output embedding dimensionality H.

Example:

>>> te = TokenEmbedding(c_in=64, llm_hidden_size=256)
>>> x = torch.randn(4, 16, 64)  # [B, N, P*C]
>>> te(x).shape
torch.Size([4, 16, 256])
forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Embeds input tokens via circular convolution.

Permutes the input to [B, C_in, N] for Conv1d, applies the convolution, then transposes back to [B, N, H].

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Patch tokens of shape [B, N, P*C].

Returns:

Embedded tokens of shape [B, N, H].

Return type:

torch.Tensor

layers.token_prototype_embedding¶

Module for Token Prototype Embedding.

This module provides a mechanism to project a large vocabulary embedding into a smaller embedding space using a linear projection.

Example

>>> vocab_size, small_vocab_size, embedding_dim = 1000, 100, 512
>>> word_embedding = torch.randn(vocab_size, embedding_dim)
>>> model = TokenPrototypeEmbedding(vocab_size, small_vocab_size, word_embedding)
>>> projected_embedding = model()
>>> print(projected_embedding.shape)
torch.Size([1000, 100])

References

Paper

Jin, Ming; Wang, Shiyu; Ma, Lintao; Chu, Zhixuan; Zhang, James Y.; Shi, Xiaoming; Chen, Pin-Yu; Liang, Yuxuan; Li, Yuan-Fang; Pan, Shirui; Wen, Qingsong: Time-LLM: Time Series Forecasting by Reprogramming Large Language Models, in: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations, 2024

Source Code

https://github.com/KimMeen/Time-LLM/blob/main/models/TimeLLM.py

class layers.token_prototype_embedding.TokenPrototypeEmbedding(vocab_size: int, small_vocab_size: int, word_embedding: Tensor)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Token Prototype Embedding Module.

Projects a large vocabulary embedding into a smaller embedding space.

vocab_size¶

Size of the original vocabulary.

small_vocab_size¶

Size of the target (smaller) vocabulary.

linear_projection¶

Linear layer for projection.

word_embedding¶

Original word embedding tensor.

forward() → Tensor[source]¶

Projects the word embedding into the smaller vocabulary space.

Returns:

Projected embedding tensor of shape (vocab_size, small_vocab_size).

Return type:

torch.Tensor
